points.text: Check gateway logs for error patterns and response times to identify failing requests.
Enable distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry) to see latency across downstream calls.
Monitor per‑service metrics (CPU, memory, request rate) to spot spikes after the release.
Profile database queries; look for slow or high‑cost statements causing CPU spikes.
Review recent code changes for added downstream calls or increased request concurrency.
Temporarily increase DB connection pool size or add read replicas to relieve CPU pressure.
Add circuit breakers around downstream services to prevent cascading failures.
Implement request timeout and retry policies to avoid hanging calls that trigger 502/504.
OUT_OF_SCOPE: Full gateway/network analysis is beyond SQL domain.
Collect DB metrics: CPU usage, wait events, active sessions, query latency.
Identify top CPU-consuming queries via pg_stat_statements or equivalent.
Examine execution plans for missing indexes or inefficient joins.
Check for lock contention: pg_locks, deadlocks, long-running transactions.
Review isolation level; consider READ COMMITTED to reduce locking overhead.
Enable connection pooling; ensure max connections do not exceed CPU capacity.
Profile downstream service latency; add tracing to correlate DB spikes with calls.
Check gateway logs for 502/504 patterns and timeout settings to confirm if errors stem from upstream latency.
Enable detailed tracing on the backend to measure latency of each downstream HTTP/TCP call.
Correlate DB CPU spikes with specific query types; use EXPLAIN to identify slow queries.
Monitor connection pool usage on the backend and DB to detect exhaustion or queuing.
Temporarily increase gateway timeout thresholds to see if errors disappear, indicating latency issues.
Roll back the recent release to a known good version to isolate if code changes introduced bottlenecks.
Profile backend thread/CPU usage to see if high concurrency or blocking I/O is causing overload.
Implement circuit breakers or retries with back‑off for downstream services to prevent cascading failures.
OUT_OF_SCOPE: The query pertains to system troubleshooting, not within the math_logic domain.
UNKNOWN: Unable to provide a precise answer due to domain mismatch.
OUT_OF_SCOPE
UNKNOWN: The request concerns performance troubleshooting, which is outside the security_defensive domain scope.
